_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-14 Hemorrhagic Shock (Revised 05/23/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-14: Hemorrhagic Shock — MDHHS Approved 5/23/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Hemorrhagic Shock Management
points: Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol and General Trauma-Treatment Protocol
Use for signs and symptoms of shock attributed to hemorrhage, including trauma and severe postpartum hemorrhage
Control bleeding per Bleeding Control (BCON)-Treatment Protocol
Transport promptly; no intervention should delay transport
For hypotension without moderate/severe head trauma: Adults receive NS or LR bolus up to 1 L; Pediatrics receive NS or LR 20 mL/kg
If moderate or severe head trauma is present, use the Head Injury Moderate and Severe TBI protocol fluid guidelines
Consider other traumatic causes of shock, including tension pneumothorax requiring Pleural Decompression and neurogenic shock requiring Shock protocol

sections.title: Tranexamic Acid (TXA)
points: TXA is per MCA selection and is considered when bleeding is uncontrolled and non-compressible
Eligibility: age greater than 18 years, weight greater than 50 kg, and destination hospital capable of administering the second dose
Dose: 1 gram TXA in 100 mL NS infused over 10 minutes IV
Use a filter needle if drawing from an ampule and label the medication bag
Medical Control or receiving hospital must be verbally notified before arrival; report TXA administration to the ED and document it
Medical Control may order TXA for selected suspected compensated shock cases that do not meet standard criteria

sections.title: Transport
points: Additional fluid boluses may be given up to adult maximum 2 L or pediatric maximum 40 mL/kg
Monitor for pulmonary edema; stop fluids and contact Medical Control if pulmonary edema develops
Transport to appropriate trauma center per MCA Transport Protocol
Pre-notify receiving facility: mechanism, estimated blood loss, current vitals, hemorrhage control interventions, IV access, fluids given, TXA administration if applicable

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether TXA is authorized for field use in hemorrhagic shock. If Yes: standard eligibility includes age > 18 years, weight > 50 kg, uncontrolled non-compressible bleeding, and destination capable of administering the second dose.

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO (Hemorrhagic Shock)
key_drugs.dose: Adults: NS or LR bolus up to 1 L; Pediatrics: 20 mL/kg; additional boluses up to adult max 2 L or pediatric max 40 mL/kg
key_drugs.contraindications: Use Head Injury Moderate and Severe TBI protocol fluid guidelines when moderate/severe head trauma is present
key_drugs.side_effects: Dilutional coagulopathy, pulmonary edema with excessive volumes
key_drugs.notes: No intervention should delay transport. Monitor for pulmonary edema and stop fluids/contact Medical Control if it develops.

key_drugs.name: Tranexamic Acid (TXA)
key_drugs.dose: 1 gram in 100 mL NS infused over 10 minutes IV
key_drugs.indication: Uncontrolled non-compressible hemorrhage per MCA selection — standard eligibility age > 18 and weight > 50 kg
key_drugs.contraindications: Age ≤ 18 or weight ≤ 50 kg unless specifically ordered by Medical Control; destination must be capable of second dose
key_drugs.side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, hypotension if administered too rapidly; theoretical thrombotic risk
key_drugs.notes: Medical Control or receiving hospital must be verbally notified before arrival. Label the medication bag, report to ED, and document administration.

deterioration_flags: Uncontrolled bleeding — apply Bleeding Control protocol and transport promptly
Moderate/severe head trauma with hemorrhagic shock — use Head Injury TBI fluid targets rather than generic hemorrhagic shock targets
Need for additional fluid beyond adult 2 L or pediatric 40 mL/kg maximum — contact Medical Control
Pulmonary edema during fluid administration — stop fluids and contact Medical Control
Uncontrolled non-compressible hemorrhage meeting MCA criteria — consider TXA and notify Medical Control/receiving hospital before arrival
scope_notes: Hemorrhage control, tourniquet, and wound packing: all levels
IV/IO access (two large bore sites if possible): Specialist skill
Fluid resuscitation with NS or LR: Specialist skill
TXA administration: Specialist skill — per MCA selection (Yes/No)
Medical Control or receiving hospital verbal notification for TXA: required before arrival
